title: Credits
text:
Project Note
This repository republishes transformed public data and documentation to make the source easier to use. It is an independent open-source project and is not an official Sundhedsdatastyrelsen publication.

title: Usage
text: This page shows a few small, practical ways to use the published outputs.

title: Usage
text:
Download The Current Release
The links below are constructed from the latest generated release tag. They point to the release assets produced by the workflow.


Latest release links will appear here after the page loads.

title: Usage
section: Example: Read The Long CSV In Pandas
text:
Example: Read The Long CSV In Pandas
import pandas as pd

release_tag = "ruks-2025-11-28"
date_part = release_tag.replace("ruks-", "")
url = (
    "https://github.com/steenhulthin/ruks-data/releases/download/"
    f"{release_tag}/ruks_hovedresultater_long-{date_part}.csv.gz"
)

df = pd.read_csv(url)

asthma = df[
    (df["disease_label"] == "astma")
    & (df["measure_code"] == "incidence")
    & (df["geo_level"] == "country")
    & (df["sex_code"] == "both")
    & (df["age_group_code"] == "alle-aldre")
    & (df["value_kind"] == "count")
]

print(asthma[["year", "value"]].head())

title: Usage
text:
Example: Query The SQLite Snapshot
import sqlite3
import pandas as pd

conn = sqlite3.connect("ruks-2025-11-28.sqlite")

sql = """
select
  f.year,
  f.value
from fact_observation f
join dim_disease d on d.disease_id = f.disease_id
join dim_measure m on m.measure_id = f.measure_id
join dim_geography g on g.geography_id = f.geography_id
join dim_sex s on s.sex_id = f.sex_id
join dim_age_group a on a.age_group_id = f.age_group_id
join dim_unit u on u.unit_id = f.unit_id
where d.disease_slug = 'astma'
  and m.measure_code = 'incidence'
  and g.geo_level = 'country'
  and s.sex_code = 'both'
  and a.age_group_code = 'alle-aldre'
  and u.unit_code = 'count__persons__none'
order by f.year
"""

df = pd.read_sql_query(sql, conn)
print(df)

title: Usage
text:
Example: Static-Site Preview
Because the site is static, it does not load the full dataset into the browser. Instead, it uses a small generated JSON summary. The table below is rendered in the browser from site/data/latest-summary.json and shows national asthma incidence counts and rates.


Example series preview will appear here after the page loads.

title: RUKS Data
text: This site publishes open, reusable outputs derived from the Danish RUKS workbook.

title: RUKS Data
text:
What You Get

A reproducible transformation of the Hovedresultater sheet.
Release-ready artifacts for SQLite, Parquet, and long-format CSV.
Lightweight JSON for browsing the latest release on GitHub Pages.
Manifests and release history tracked in Git.

title: RUKS Data
text:
Latest Release
The summary below is filled from the generated site/data/latest-summary.json file after the update workflow runs.


Run the workflow once to populate the latest-release panel.

title: RUKS Data
text:
Outputs

Git-tracked metadata: data/manifests/*.json and data/history/releases.csv
Site data: site/data/latest-summary.json
Release assets: long CSV, Parquet, and SQLite snapshots attached to GitHub Releases

title: RUKS Data
text:
Using The Data
The site now includes a dedicated usage page with copy-pasteable examples for Python and a small static preview built from the generated summary JSON:

Usage

title: RUKS Data
text:
Notes On Missing Values
This publication contains many blank cells in small geographic and demographic slices. The current pipeline preserves those blanks as missing values rather than forcing 0. We expect that at least part of this pattern reflects disclosure control for small cells, but that interpretation should be verified against the source publication and related documentation.

title: Methodology
text: The current pipeline processes the Hovedresultater worksheet from the public RUKS workbook.

title: Methodology
text: The current pipeline processes the Hovedresultater worksheet from the public RUKS workbook.

title: Methodology
text:
Normalization
Each populated year cell becomes one observation in a long-format fact table with:

measure
disease
geography level
region
municipality
sex
age group
value kind
standardization
year
value

title: Methodology
text:
Missing Data
Blank cells in the workbook are preserved as missing and are not coerced to 0.

title: Methodology
text:
Publication Strategy

small manifests and history logs are committed to Git
large artifacts are produced in artifacts/ and intended for GitHub Releases
the Quarto site consumes small generated JSON summaries
